2XClient.Title: Acquired by Parallels and discontinued › Now continues as Parallels Client
2XClient.Notice:
2X Client was acquired by Parallels and discontinued under that name. It lives on as Parallels Client, which is the actively maintained successor.

The version available here is no longer maintained and receives no security updates.

aMSNPortable.Title: Discontinued application › No longer receives security updates
aMSNPortable.Notice: This application has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network or web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches, meaning any internet-connected use carries real unpatched-vulnerability risk.

AudacityPortable.Title: Telemetry and vendor update checks added after the 2021 acquisition
AudacityPortable.Notice:
When Audacity was acquired by Muse Group in 2021, the application changed its behavior in ways worth knowing about. A revised privacy policy introduced limited telemetry (collecting your IP address, operating system, and CPU type) along with an automatic update check that contacts the vendor. The policy also referenced occasional data sharing with offices in Russia.

This is a behavioral change from the original offline-only tool. It is not malware, but it is no longer the silent, network-isolated application it once was.

balenaEtcherPortable.Title: Collects usage analytics and contacts the vendor by default
balenaEtcherPortable.Notice: balenaEtcher sends usage analytics to the vendor and contacts its servers by default. This is not malicious behavior, but the application does phone home unless you go into the settings and turn analytics off.

BitcoinPortable.Title: Full-node cryptocurrency wallet › Outdated versions are a financial risk
BitcoinPortable.Notice:
This is a full-node cryptocurrency wallet. The software itself is not malicious, but if you store real funds here, keeping it up to date really matters. Older node and wallet releases have had important security and consensus-related fixes, and running an outdated version with a real balance is a genuine financial risk.

Always run the latest release and keep an offline backup of your wallet and keys.

CamStudioPortable.Title: Abandoned screen recorder › Official installer repeatedly flagged for adware and trojans
CamStudioPortable.Notice:
CamStudio has been abandoned and is no longer maintained. Its official installer was repeatedly flagged by antivirus software for bundled adware and trojan detections.

The portable build here bypasses the official installer, so those components are not included.

ccPortable.Title: Piriform product owned by Avast › 2017 supply-chain attack and default telemetry
ccPortable.Notice:
This is a Piriform product, now owned by Avast (Gen Digital). In 2017, CCleaner's official build was compromised in a supply-chain attack that distributed malware to roughly 2.27 million users. A further intrusion attempt was reported in 2019.

Recent Piriform builds also enable telemetry by default and include upsell prompts.

ConsolePortable.Title: Discontinued and succeeded by the ConsoleZ fork
ConsolePortable.Notice: Console2 has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. Its active fork ConsoleZ is available as a replacement, though it is not currently offered here.

DetectItEasyPortable.Title: Legitimate reverse-engineering tool › Antivirus may raise a false-positive
DetectItEasyPortable.Notice:
Detect It Easy is a legitimate tool used for reverse engineering and binary analysis. Because tools like this are also used by malware authors, antivirus software may occasionally flag it as suspicious.

This is a false-positive. The tool is safe to use.

DiffpdfPortable.Title: Free version discontinued › Now available under a commercial license only
DiffpdfPortable.Notice: The open-source version of diffpdf has been discontinued. The application continues, but only under a commercial license.

DVDStylerPortable.Title: Official installer bundled adware during its SourceForge era
DVDStylerPortable.Notice:
DVDStyler's official installer bundled adware during its time on SourceForge. The portable build here bypasses the official installer, so those components are not included.

The application itself is legitimate, though it receives only light maintenance.

EvincePortable.Title: Windows build discontinued › Upstream continues on Linux only
EvincePortable.Notice: The independent Windows build of Evince has been discontinued. The upstream project remains active on Linux, but the version available here (2.32.0) dates from 2014 and will not receive any further updates.

FalkonPortable.Title: Discontinued browser with an unpatched web engine
FalkonPortable.Notice: Falkon has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It runs on an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches, which means general browsing carries real unpatched-vulnerability risk.

FileZillaPortable.Title: Client is clean and open source › Official installer bundled adware for years
FileZillaPortable.Notice:
The FileZilla client itself is clean and open source. However, its official installer bundled third-party adware for years.

The portable build here bypasses the official installer entirely, so those bundled components are not included.

FotografixPortable.Title: Free version frozen › Successor is no longer available as freeware
FotografixPortable.Notice: The Fotografix Classic free version (1.x) has been frozen and will not receive further updates. Later versions (Fotografix 2026 and beyond) are no longer available as freeware.

FreeDownloadManagerPortable.Title: Vendor distribution channel was compromised between 2020 and 2022
FreeDownloadManagerPortable.Notice:
Free Download Manager's official website was compromised between 2020 and 2022 and selectively served a backdoored Linux build containing an information stealer. This was confirmed by Kaspersky and later acknowledged by the vendor. Windows builds were not directly affected.

The incident shows that this vendor's distribution channel has been compromised in the past. If the package retrieves files from the vendor during installation, the integrity of those files depends on that channel. It is a good idea to verify download hashes.

FreeFileSyncPortable.Title: Severely outdated version › Current release is 14.9
FreeFileSyncPortable.Notice: The version available here (6.2, from 2014) is severely outdated. FreeFileSync is actively maintained and is currently at version 14.9.

FreeUPXPortable.Title: Executable packer › Packed files may trigger antivirus heuristics
FreeUPXPortable.Notice: FUPX is a legitimate executable compressor based on UPX. Because packed executables often trigger antivirus heuristics, both the tool itself and the files it produces may occasionally be flagged as false-positives.

fscPortable.Title: Became a commercial product from version 5.4 › This is the last freeware release (5.3)
fscPortable.Notice: FastStone Capture became a commercial product from version 5.4 onwards. The version available here (5.3) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

GnumericPortable.Title: Windows build outdated › Upstream development has not kept pace
GnumericPortable.Notice: The Gnumeric Windows build available here (1.12.17, from 2014) is outdated. The upstream project continues at a slow pace, but Windows builds have not kept up with newer releases.

HMNISEditPortable.Title: Discontinued around 2005 › No support for NSIS 3
HMNISEditPortable.Notice: HM NIS Edit has been discontinued since approximately 2005 and does not support NSIS 3. It is no longer a practical editor for modern NSIS scripts.

IcoFXPortable.Title: Became a commercial product from version 2.0 › This is the last freeware release (1.6.4)
IcoFXPortable.Notice: IcoFX became a commercial product from version 2.0 onwards. The version available here (1.6.4) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

ImgBurnPortable.Title: Abandoned disc-burning tool › Official installer historically bundled adware
ImgBurnPortable.Notice:
ImgBurn is no longer maintained. Its last release was in 2013. Its official installer historically bundled adware (OpenCandy).

The portable build here bypasses the official installer, so those components are not included.

InstantbirdPortable.Title: Discontinued Mozilla-based instant messaging client
InstantbirdPortable.Notice: Instantbird has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

IObitUninstallerPortable.Title: Vendor with a reputation for aggressive bundling and upselling
IObitUninstallerPortable.Notice:
IObit products have a long-standing reputation for aggressive bundling and upselling. The company was also accused in 2009 of incorporating a competitor's malware detection database without authorization.

The tools are functional, but the vendor's practices are worth being aware of.

IronPortable.Title: Unofficial Chromium fork › Privacy claims have been criticized as overstated
IronPortable.Notice:
SRWare Iron is an unofficial Chromium fork. It handles all your web traffic, relies on a small team for security updates, and has long been criticized for overstating its privacy advantages over standard Chromium.

No malicious behavior has been documented. The main consideration is how much you trust the team and how quickly they ship security patches.

JkDefragPortable.Title: Discontinued with no active successor
JkDefragPortable.Notice: JkDefrag has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. Its intended successor, MyDefrag, has also since been abandoned, leaving no active continuation of the project.

KasperskyTDSSKillerPortable.Title: Russia-based vendor subject to government bans and warnings › Tool is also discontinued
KasperskyTDSSKillerPortable.Notice:
This is a Kaspersky tool. In 2022, Germany's Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) warned against using Kaspersky products. In 2024, the United States banned their sale and distribution over concerns about potential data access by Russian authorities.

The tool itself is also discontinued and no longer receives updates.

KeePassProPortable.Title: CVE-2023-32784 was fixed in version 2.54 › Please make sure you are on 2.54 or later
KeePassProPortable.Notice:
KeePass 2.x (the Professional edition) was affected by CVE-2023-32784, a vulnerability that could allow an attacker with access to the machine to recover the master password from a process memory dump. This was fixed in version 2.54.

Please make sure you are running version 2.54 or later. KeePass Classic (1.x) and KeePassXC were not affected by this vulnerability.

KompoZerPortable.Title: Discontinued WYSIWYG web editor › Ships an unpatched web-rendering engine
KompoZerPortable.Notice: KompoZer is a Mozilla-based WYSIWYG web editor that was discontinued around 2010. It ships an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches. Its intended successor, BlueGriffon, has also since been abandoned.

MaxthonPortable.Title: Documented to transmit browsing data to servers in China
MaxthonPortable.Notice:
Maxthon is developed in China. Independent security researchers at Fidelis Cybersecurity and Exatel found that it transmits sensitive data to a server in Beijing, including your full browsing history, search queries, operating system details, and a list of installed applications. This continued even when the User Experience Improvement Program was disabled.

More recent reporting from 2024 to 2025 indicates that data is still being sent to servers in China. This is browser-level behavior and cannot be addressed by portable packaging.

MediaInfoPortable.Title: App is clean › Only some official GUI installers historically included bundled adware
MediaInfoPortable.Notice: MediaInfo itself is clean and open source. Only certain official GUI installer builds offered bundled adware at some point. The command-line version and portable builds are not affected.

MirandaPortable.Title: Discontinued instant messaging client
MirandaPortable.Notice: Miranda IM has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

Notepad2Portable.Title: Discontinued text editor › Succeeded by Notepad3
Notepad2Portable.Notice: Notepad2 and its mod fork have both been discontinued and are no longer maintained. Notepad3 is the active successor and a recommended replacement.

OperaMailPortable.Title: Discontinued Opera email client
OperaMailPortable.Notice: Opera Mail has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

OperaPortable.Title: China-based ownership since 2016 › Predatory loan-app scandal and browser telemetry
OperaPortable.Notice:
Opera has been owned by a China-based consortium (with Kunlun Tech holding the majority stake) since 2016. In 2020, investigative reporting found that the company also operated predatory mobile loan apps that harvested users' contacts and charged extreme interest rates.

The desktop browser includes telemetry.

OperaPortableLegacy12.Title: Legacy Presto-era Opera › No longer receives any security updates
OperaPortableLegacy12.Notice: This is a legacy Opera build using the discontinued Presto rendering engine, which no longer receives any security updates. It can be useful for compatibility or testing purposes, but it is not suitable for general or sensitive web browsing.

PDF-XChangeViewerPortable.Title: Discontinued and superseded by PDF-XChange Editor
PDF-XChangeViewerPortable.Notice: PDF-XChange Viewer has been discontinued and no longer receives security updates. It has been superseded by PDF-XChange Editor. As an unpatched PDF renderer, it could be vulnerable when opening documents from untrusted sources.

PhotoFilmStripPortable.Title: License changed from version 3.1.2 › The free version line is now frozen
PhotoFilmStripPortable.Notice: PhotoFilmStrip changed its license from version 3.1.2 onwards. The free version line has been frozen and will not receive further updates.

ProcessHackerPortable.Title: Renamed and superseded by System Informer
ProcessHackerPortable.Notice: Process Hacker has been renamed to System Informer and is now fully open source. System Informer is the recommended replacement and is available here.

qBittorrentEnhancedPortable.Title: Third-party qBittorrent fork with additional network behavior
qBittorrentEnhancedPortable.Notice: qBittorrent Enhanced is a third-party fork that adds behavior such as automatic peer banning. The added functionality and the fact that it is independently maintained are both worth considering from a trust perspective.

QupZillaPortable.Title: Discontinued browser › Succeeded by Falkon, which has also been discontinued
QupZillaPortable.Notice: QupZilla has been discontinued and was succeeded by Falkon, which has since also been discontinued. It ships an outdated web-rendering engine that no longer receives security patches.

SeaMonkeyPortable.Title: Older Gecko engine › Web security fixes arrive slowly or not at all
SeaMonkeyPortable.Notice: SeaMonkey is built on an older version of the Gecko engine and lags well behind Firefox, meaning web security fixes arrive slowly or not at all. It is fine for local use, but it is not suitable for general or sensitive web browsing.

SmartDeblurPortable.Title: Became a commercial product from version 2.0 › This is the last freeware release (1.27)
SmartDeblurPortable.Notice: Smart Deblur became a commercial product from version 2.0 onwards. The version available here (1.27) is the last release that was distributed as freeware.

SUMoPortable.Title: Discontinued KC Softwares utility › Installers historically bundled third-party offers
SUMoPortable.Notice: This KC Softwares utility has been discontinued. The vendor's installers historically bundled third-party offers, and the tool itself is unmaintained. It checks for software updates by contacting the internet.

SunbirdPortable.Title: Discontinued Mozilla calendar application
SunbirdPortable.Notice: Mozilla Sunbird has been discontinued and is no longer maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

SupermiumPortable.Title: Unofficial browser fork › Maintained by a small independent team
SupermiumPortable.Notice:
This is an unofficial, independently maintained fork of a mainstream browser. It handles all your web traffic and depends on a small team to keep pace with upstream security patches.

No malicious behavior has been documented. The main consideration is trust and how quickly patches are shipped.

SylpheedPortable.Title: Discontinued email client
SylpheedPortable.Notice: Sylpheed has been discontinued and is no longer actively maintained. It ships an outdated network engine that no longer receives security patches.

SystemExplorerPortable.Title: Discontinued since around 2016
SystemExplorerPortable.Notice: System Explorer has been discontinued and has not been maintained since approximately 2016.

SystemInformerPortable.Title: Legitimate advanced system tool › Kernel driver may trigger antivirus alerts
SystemInformerPortable.Notice:
System Informer is a legitimate, advanced tool for monitoring processes and system activity. Its kernel driver is sometimes abused by malware to disable security software, which can cause antivirus products to flag it as potentially unwanted.

The tool is safe when obtained from a trusted source.

TeamViewerPortable.Title: Legitimate remote access tool › Frequently abused in technical-support scams
TeamViewerPortable.Notice:
TeamViewer is legitimate remote-access software, but it is one of the most commonly abused tools in technical-support scams. Remote-access tools are also a frequent intrusion vector, and TeamViewer was in fact the entry point in the 2017 CCleaner supply-chain breach.

Only ever grant a connection to someone you contacted first and fully trust.

UltraDefragPortable.Title: Open-source version discontinued › Now available under a commercial license only
UltraDefragPortable.Notice: The open-source version of UltraDefrag has been discontinued. The application continues, but only under a commercial license.

uTorrentPortable.Title: History of bundled adware › Included a hidden cryptocurrency miner in 2015
uTorrentPortable.Notice:
In 2015, uTorrent's official installer was found to include a cryptocurrency miner called EpicScale without clear user consent. The installer has also had a long history of bundling adware.

The torrent client itself is functional. The portable build here bypasses the official installer, so those bundled components are not included.

VirtualDubPortable.Title: Discontinued since around 2013 › Active fork VirtualDub2 is not offered here
VirtualDubPortable.Notice: VirtualDub has been discontinued and has not been maintained since approximately 2013. An active fork called VirtualDub2 exists as a replacement, though it is not currently offered here.

WackGetPortable.Title: Discontinued download manager
WackGetPortable.Notice: WackGet has been discontinued and is no longer maintained.

wxDownloadFastPortable.Title: Discontinued download manager
wxDownloadFastPortable.Notice: wxDownload Fast has been discontinued and is no longer maintained.

ZSoftUninstallerPortable.Title: Discontinued since around 2010
ZSoftUninstallerPortable.Notice: ZSoft Uninstaller has been discontinued and has not been maintained since approximately 2010.
